name: Straits of the Ebron River and Calicanto Waterfall from El Cuervo
name_original: Estrechos del Río Ebron y Cascada de Calicanto desde El Cuervo
region_intro: Hiking around El Cuervo offers diverse trails through a landscape shaped by the Ebrón River. The region is characterized by river gorges, canyons, and natural rock formations, providing varied terrain for exploration. These hiking trails in El Cuervo often lead to scenic spots like waterfalls and natural bridges. The area provides a mix of moderate and easy routes suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around El Cuervo The most popular hiking route is Straits of the Ebron River and Calicanto Waterfall from El Cuervo, a 9.5 miles (15.2 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete, featuring the Ebron River and Calicanto Waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Ebrón Canyon Trail – Gorges of the Ebrón River loop from El Cuervo, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route explores the Río Ebrón Canyon and its impressive gorges. Local hikers also love the Natural Bridge Over the Gorge – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Río Ebrón, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) easy trail leading through a landscape with a natural bridge and waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around El Cuervo is defined by river canyons, gorges, and waterfalls, offering a mix of natural beauty and varied terrain. The network provides options for both easy family friendly hikes and more challenging moderate routes. The routes in El Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore El Cuervo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around El Cuervo?
A: El Cuervo offers a diverse range of hiking trails, primarily characterized by river gorges, canyons, and natural rock formations shaped by the Ebrón River. You'll find a mix of easy and moderate routes, often leading to scenic spots like waterfalls and natural bridges. There are 10 routes in total, with an even split of 5 easy and 5 moderate options.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in El Cuervo?
A: Yes, El Cuervo has 5 easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed walk. One popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40345198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Bridge Over the Gorge – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Río Ebrón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km long and typically takes around 2 hours to complete, featuring a natural bridge and a waterfall.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails in El Cuervo?
A: The trails around El Cuervo are rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see impressive features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Ebrón River&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and unique geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Bridge Over the Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also feature the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge and Walkway of the Estrechos del Río Ebrón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in El Cuervo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in El Cuervo are designed as loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Ebrón Canyon Trail – Gorges of the Ebrón River loop from El Cuervo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.7 km path that explores the impressive canyon.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Cuervo?
A: The best times to hike in El Cuervo are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, while winter can bring colder temperatures, though some trails remain accessible.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in El Cuervo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in rural areas of Spain, including El Cuervo, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check for specific local regulations or signage at trailheads, particularly in protected natural areas, to ensure compliance.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in El Cuervo?
A: Hikes in El Cuervo vary in length and duration. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Streamside Picnic Spot loop from Torrebaja&lt;/a&gt;, can be around 5 km and take less than 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974465351/straits-of-the-ebron-river-and-calicanto-waterfall-from-el-cuervo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Straits of the Ebron River and Calicanto Waterfall from El Cuervo&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 15 km and take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Cuervo?
A: The hiking trails around El Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning river canyons, the beauty of the waterfalls, and the varied terrain that offers both natural beauty and engaging routes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hikes around El Cuervo?
A: Yes, waterfalls are a prominent feature of the hiking landscape in El Cuervo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable attraction, and several routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Ebrón River – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Tormón&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate it into their paths.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in El Cuervo?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, El Cuervo is a rural area, and it's generally possible to find parking near the start points of popular trails or within the village itself. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in El Cuervo?
A: For hiking in El Cuervo, it's best to wear comfortable, layered clothing suitable for the season. Sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential, especially on trails that involve rocky terrain or river crossings. Don't forget sun protection, water, and snacks, as some routes can be several hours long.
